codeReview.summary: The feature covers mnemonic normalization, invalid-phrase handling, and privacy treatment, but the successful restore path does not persist the rehydrated onboarding/session flags. That makes the restored state non-durable across a cold start.

issues.description: On success the screen mutates `hasCompletedOnboarding`/`hasIdentity` via `useSessionStore.setState(...)`, but `session-store.ts` only persists those fields when `completeOnboarding()` or `setHasIdentity()` call `persistSession(...)` (lines 182-200). After a restore, a kill/relaunch can rehydrate stale pre-restore values from `session:state`, and `AppNavigator` uses `hasIdentity` as `vaultInitialized` when choosing the route. This can misroute a freshly restored wallet back into onboarding/uninitialized flows instead of keeping it in the restored state.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker did not follow the mobile-feature-worker doc-loading procedure for this vault/UX feature. The skill requires reading `.factory/library/architecture.md` and `user-testing.md` for vault-touching, anchor-heavy work, but the transcript skeleton only shows reads for `mission.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `validation-contract.md`, and `.factory/services.yaml`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl session 6b2283c7-7ad7-4714-b57b-62a697928078: transcript skeleton contains `mission.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `validation-contract.md`, and `.factory/services.yaml`, but no `architecture.md` or `user-testing.md` reads, even though the feature changed `src/lib/enbox/agent-store.ts` and added anchor/privacy tests in `src/features/auth/screens/recovery-restore-screen.test.tsx`.

summary: Review failed. The restore flow sets the post-restore session flags only in memory, so the restored state is lost on the next hydrate/relaunch; I also noted a skill-procedure gap in the worker transcript.
